multidisciplinary research methods, the unique contributions Black knowledge traditions have made to these methods/their value in researching key questions of Black American, African and African diasporic life, and a practical understanding of how to apply these methods to redressing forms of social inequality.

4. Effectively develop and compose sound research-backed arguments rooted in the ﬁeld of African American and Africana Studies in oral and written form.

5. Be able to identify, describe, understand at a material level, and

propose solutions to issues of inequity, oppression and life outcomes associated with the impact of systemic racism and the intersection of multiple identities on members of the African American, African and African diaspora communities in the US and globally.

REQUIREMENTS Students must earn a grade of "C-" or better in each course that is to be counted toward completion of degree requirements. Students must have a minimum 2.0 cumulative grade point average across all courses used to satisfy major degree requirements. All related or supporting courses in other departments must be approved by an AAAS academic advisor. Each semester there will be an approved list of courses for both those choosing the major or the concentration in Public Policy. The academic advisor will review the student's options during their mandatory advising appointment.
